audit.note: The 10-year lease dated 2024-01-11 is the latest term-bearing document and, with the DB commencement of 2024-10-24, is consistent with a 2034-10-31 expiration, but the summaries do not state the exact commencement/expiration dates or option details, so a document read is advised.

Lease dated 1/9/26 states a 10-year initial term commencing 2026-01-09 (expiring ~2036-01-08) with two unexercised 5-year options; DB expiration is blank, and the exact expiration date and option notice deadlines should be confirmed by reading the lease PDF.

Original lease ran to 2017-01-31; the 2016 exercise extended through 2022-01-31, and the 1-27-23 letter exercises a further renewal option â if a 5-year term commencing 2023-02-01 this would run to 2028-01-31 (with a gap 2022â2023 suggesting an unseen extension), which conflicts with DB's 2023-08-01/2028-07-31 term, so the actual letter and any missing amendment should be read.

The document summaries never state a term length or expiration date â the 2006 ground lease and 7/7/06 memorandum summaries omit term details, the 2020 amendment is only a COVID rent deferral, and the 2021 assignment to KBP Inspired does not appear to modify the term â so the DB expiration of 2026-12-31 cannot be confirmed without reading the actual lease and assignment documents.

Third Amendment (9/11/25) extends the term ten years beyond the 2nd Amendment's 2026-04-30 expiration, i.e., through 2036-04-30, matching the DB; summary is truncated so option/notice details (DB shows 1 option, notice 2035-04-30) should be verified in the PDF.

The 1-24-26 First Amendment revises the Rent Commencement (summary truncated), so the exact commencement/expiration cannot be confirmed from summaries; DB shows 2036-03-31 expiration which implies a term based on a 2026 rent commencement, but the amendment PDF must be read to verify the revised dates.

Tenant's 7-26-21 exercise notice set the Second Additional Term at 2022-02-01 through 2027-01-31, and the July 2022 Third Amendment/Settlement resolved the landlord's challenge to that exercise; the amendment summary is truncated so it should be read to confirm it did not alter the term or remaining options.

The Fourth Amendment (executed 12-29-2023) extends the term from 2028-10-31 to 2033-10-31, superseding the DB expiration of 2026-10-31 (which reflects only the Second Amendment) and the Third Amendment's 2028-10-31.

First Amendment (1-21-21) extended term to 2027-01-31 and granted one 5-year renewal option, which tenant exercised via the 6-10-26 notice, extending the term to 2032-01-31; confidence is medium because the 2026 notice summary is truncated and does not explicitly state the new expiration date.

audit.source: Assignment & 2nd AMD AGR-Slice of NY Pizza (5-3-21).pdf
audit.note: The latest term-bearing document is the Second Amendment & Assignment (5-3-21), but its summary is truncated and does not state a new expiration; the First Amendment (10-12-16) only extended through 2021-12-31, so the DB expiration of 2028-12-31 (implying the 2nd Amendment extended the term) cannot be verified without reading the PDF.
Second Amendment dated 2026-05-05 extends the term 10 years through 2037-01-31, matching the database expiration; summary is truncated so option details are unverified.
No documents were provided on file; the database expiration of 2031-11-30 cannot be verified against any executed lease or amendment, so a document review is needed.

The 8/25/22 lease provides a 15-year term with three 5-year options, consistent with the DB commencement of 2023-07-31 implying expiration ~2038-07-30/31, but the 12/2/22 letter agreement modifying the lease and the 2024 termination of the OLD 2008 lease's MOL should be read to confirm no term change; expiration is inferred, not directly stated in summaries.

Fourth Amendment (executed 12-28-2023) extends the term three years from 2024-11-01 through 2027-10-31, matching the database; prior amendments eliminated all renewal options and no non-renewal or termination notice is on file.
The Fourth Amendment (executed 5-13-25) extends the term 120 months from 2026-05-01 through 2036-04-30, superseding the DB expiration of 2026-04-30; renewal option details not stated in summaries.
Latest executed notice (10-14-25) exercises a 5-year extension commencing 2026-11-01 and ending 2031-10-31, matching the DB expiration, with one 5-year option remaining.

Original lease (2-26-2019) expired 2024-01-31 per estoppel; tenant's 3-10-2023 letter exercised its single five-year renewal option, extending the term to 2029-01-31, matching the DB expiration, but the DB still lists the option as unexercised.

First Amendment (executed 1-12-21) extends the term 120 months from 2026-03-01 through 2036-02-29, superseding the original 10-year lease; the 2021 assignment to ACT Knightdale does not alter the term.
The Second Amendment (executed 2024-08-16) extends the term five years from 2025-04-01 through 2030-03-31, matching the database; no non-renewal or termination notices on file.

No documents are on file to verify the database term (2025-03-01 to 2030-02-28) or the single unexercised 60-month renewal option, so the database values are provisionally accepted pending retrieval and review of the actual lease documents.
No documents are on file to verify; expiration 2030-07-31 and one 60-month option with 2029-11-01 notice deadline are taken from the database only and require document confirmation.

First Amendment (executed 6-25-2024) extends the term five years through 2030-01-31, matching the database; the original lease's single 5-year option was exercised via the 4-30-2019 notice, so no options appear to remain, though DB shows no option data to compare.

Third Amendment (executed 2024-06-12) extends the term 24 months, April 1, 2025 through March 31, 2027, matching the database expiration; no non-renewal or termination notices on file, and remaining options/notice deadlines are not stated in the summaries.

No documents are on file for Michaels #6725, so the DB expiration of 2033-09-30 cannot be verified against any executed lease document; a document pull/read is required.

Latest executed exercise notice (11-24-25) extends the term from 2027-01-31 by five years to 2032-01-31, superseding the 2021 extension; the 2022 landlord termination notice was tied to a reduced-occupancy dispute and evidently not effectuated given subsequent 2024/2025 tenant activity, and per the estoppel/exercise history 3 renewal options remain.
The Fifth Amendment (executed 12-19-24) extends the term 60 months from 2025-10-01 through 2030-09-30, matching the database expiration.
No documents are on file to verify; DB shows a renewal exercised with term running 2021-12-01 to 2036-11-30 and one option remaining, but this cannot be confirmed against any executed document.

No documents are on file to verify the DB expiration of 2065-06-23 (a ~59-year term from 2006-05-23 is atypical and may be a data-entry error); the lease and any amendments must be located and read.

Original 5/22/2023 lease is a 15-year term with three 5-year options; the DB commencement of 2024-03-18 (consistent with the 3/21/2024 Notice of Lease and 1st Amendment) yields expiration ~2039-03-31, but the summaries don't state the exact commencement/expiration dates so the amendment and notice should be read to confirm.

The 10-year lease (10-27-21) with rent commencement 2022-03-29 supports a 2032-03-31 expiration, but the Confirmation of Lease Term Certificate summary is truncated and does not explicitly state the expiration date, so the PDF should be read to confirm.

The original 10-year lease effective 2018-08-23 governs (expiring ~2028-08-31 by strict 10-year math but DB shows 2029-01-31, plausibly due to lease-year rounding to end of month or later tender of premises); the 2020 assignment to H10 Health Services does not alter the term, so the exact commencement/expiration mechanics should be verified in the lease PDF.

The Second Amendment (7-31-25) assigns the lease to Thrive Burrito and extends the term by ten years, consistent with the DB's 2025-09-01 to 2035-08-31 term, but the summary is truncated so the exact extension dates and option details should be verified in the PDF.

Only the original 10-year lease (executed 2025-10-16) is on file; its summary confirms a 10-year term consistent with the DB expiration of 2036-05-31 from a 2026-05-14 commencement, but the summary does not state the actual commencement/expiration dates or option details, so the PDF should be read to verify.

Tenant's 11-6-23 first renewal option exercise extends the term to 2029-10-31, matching the database; however, the landlord's 11-4-23 forfeiture letter (asserting late notice) creates ambiguity as to whether the exercise was accepted, so the notices should be read in full.

A 10-year lease commencing 2017-04-11 supports the DB expiration of 2027-04-30, but the truncated First Amendment (dated 2020-08-10) summary does not reveal whether it modified the term, so the PDFs should be read to confirm.

No documents are on file to verify the database term (commencement 2021-08-27, expiration 2031-08-31, one 60-month option with notice due 2030-08-31), so the DB values are provisionally accepted pending review of the actual lease documents.

The 11-8-21 notice exercised the SECOND 5-year renewal (following the first exercised 11-4-16), consistent with the DB expiration of 2027-07-31; however, the DB shows the renewal as unexercised and lists a 2027-02-01 notice deadline, and it is unclear whether any options remain beyond the two exercised, so the PDFs should be checked.

The 120-month lease (4/30/24) with DB commencement 2025-01-01 would suggest expiration ~2034-12-31, but three amendments (1st modifies commencement, 2nd and 3rd summaries truncated) may explain the DB's 2035-11-30 date; the truncated summaries prevent confirming the exact commencement/expiration, so the PDFs (especially the 1st and 3rd Amendments) must be read.

The 10-year lease dated 2019-03-21 with rent commencement 2019-09-02 supports the 2029-09-30 expiration (confirmed by the lease profile), but the First (9-9-20) and Second (11-21-20) Amendment summaries are truncatedâlikely COVID rent reliefâand should be read to confirm they do not modify the term.

The Second Amendment (9-14-22) relocating tenant to 3,100 SF is the latest term-bearing document and presumably sets the 2032-12-31 expiration matching the DB, but its summary is truncated so the exact expiration, option count, and notice deadline (DB shows 2037-04-01, which appears inconsistent with a 2032 expiration) should be verified by reading the amendment.
